Catharanthus roseus is a flowering plant native to Madagascar, commonly known as Catanthera endertii is not. However, Catharanthus roseus is often referred to by this name.

Catharanthus roseus is a shrub-like plant, which is valued for its medicinal properties. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Some of the health benefits of Catharanthus roseus include its ability to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which can help to fight off infections, and its potential to treat certain types of cancer. 

The bioactive constituents of Catharanthus roseus include alkaloids such as vinblastine and vincristine, which have antineoplastic properties, as well as other compounds like catharanthine and ajmalicine, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects.

The most common herbal preparations made with Catharanthus roseus include a dried root extract, a standardized leaf extract, and a tincture. These preparations are often used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and certain types of cancer.
